title: audit — subscribed events
description: Subscribes to cross-module lifecycle events (TenantErased, UserErased, IdentityMerged, TenantSuspended) for compliance-driven cascades — plus own retention-policy events for cache invalidation. Audit does NOT subscribe to arbitrary domain events (capture is via the vendor Auditable trait's observer, not via listeners).

listeners.purpose: Clears the per-tenant retention cache (audit:retention:{tenant_id}) so PurgeExpiredAuditsJob picks up the new cutoff on its next scheduled run.
Same as above — unconditional cache eviction on any policy CRUD.
Cache eviction — retention_years is now back to default; the next purge cycle will use it.
Fires AuditRetentionPolicyExtendedNotification to the tenant's compliance officer + owner. Informs them that a retention override is now active + what the extended cutoff is.
Fires AuditRetentionExtensionExpiringSoonNotification (30d ahead) + a completion notification when actually expired.

Writes a summary audit row via the vendor Auditable trait so the trail records itself being purged. Config-gated by audit.compliance.write_purge_meta_audit=true (default). Skipped in test envs to avoid noisy audit rows.
Accumulates purge counts into a Redis key (audit:purge:digest:{tenant_id}:{week_bucket}) for the weekly AuditPurgeCompletedNotification digest sent on Sundays.

Migrates the tenant's audit rows to the compliance archive (NOT cascade-delete). Regulators may need proof of erasure years later. See retention.json §TenantErased cascade — audit is deliberately different from analytics/marketing/activity.

GDPR Art. 17 cascade for the erased user_id. REDACTS old_values + new_values fields matching the erased subject's PII to a SHA-256 hash. Fact-of-mutation record (event, auditable_type, auditable_id, created_at) IS PRESERVED. Writes a summary redaction audit row referencing the erased user.

Same redaction cascade for cross-application identity erasure (Wave 6+). An Identity spans multiple User rows across applications; the redaction pass runs against every audit row for every affected user_id.

When a tenant is suspended (billing lapse, ToS violation), FREEZE the retention cutoff at now(). Suspended tenants may need extended access to their pre-suspension audit trail during dispute resolution — the freeze prevents PurgeExpiredAuditsJob from deleting rows during suspension. On tenant resume the freeze is lifted.
Lifts the retention freeze. Normal PurgeExpiredAuditsJob cadence resumes.

Emergency kill switch. When audit_capture is revoked (compliance-officer override), unbind the vendor AuditableObserver so subsequent mutations write no audit rows. Retention purge continues to run on existing rows. Requires compliance-officer approval + logged reason (writes a security audit row for the revocation).
Re-binds the vendor AuditableObserver after audit_capture is restored.
